Let’s dive in:
First, what’s the #1 HIDDEN CAUSE of an aging thyroid gland?
And why do routine blood tests never reveal it?
It’s called Iodine Resistance.1
Iodine is a mineral that your body needs in just the right amount.
The largest concentration of it is found inside your thyroid gland.
Your thyroid takes iodine found in food and converts it into 2 specific thyroid
hormones:
Thyroxine (T4) and Triiodothyronine (T3).
However, without adequate iodine inside your thyroid you cannot adequately
produce these hormones.
This is what doctors refer to as iodine resistance.
So what do these hormones do?
Think of T4 as the “Private First Class” thyroid hormone.
It’s necessary, but not in COMMAND of your metabolism.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 35 --

However, when things are working properly, your T4 is converted into the
more metabolically active Free T3.
And here’s the rub:
Free T3 is your most powerful weight loss hormone.
Free T3 doesn't bind to protein, unlike normal T3.
It circulates freely in your blood and performs metabolic, weight loss, and
energy production wonders.
Consider Free T3 The 5-Star General of your body’s metabolism.
It is the big boss when it comes to weight loss, energy production, mood
enhancement, and more.
Free T3 is like Arnold Schwarzenegger in The Terminator:
It terminates fat cells like a machine on a MISSION.
However, when you suffer from Iodine Resistance, your Free T3 levels drop…
… and even WORSE:
Your cells cannot absorb what little Free T3 is available.
When your body cannot convert enough T3 from T4, or cannot absorb it
sufficiently, it’s known as tertiary hypothyroidism.
And guess what?
Standard blood tests fail to check for this.
During your last blood test, your doctor may have quote-unquote “checked
your thyroid” by running what’s called a TSH test.
This looks at your body’s thyroid stimulating hormone.
For decades we assumed low TSH meant normal thyroid function.
But if you have Iodine Resistance, your TSH can be perfectly normal…
...yet your Free T3 levels could be dangerously low.
Your body may be producing T4, but it’s not being converted into Free T3.
That means you may be severely hypothyroid and not even know it.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

You may be thinking:
“Dr. Capasso, can’t I just take iodine as a supplement?”
While iodine is occasionally used under a doctor’s supervision, it will not
solve the problem UNLESS you get the foods and thyroid toxins out of your
system.
And there are serious concerns with iodine therapy.
For starters, iodine can be TOXIC.
Dr. Alan Gaby in an editorial published in the Townsend Letter for Doctors
and Patients, has this to say about iodine therapy:
“Fairly modest increases in iodine intake have been reported to cause
thyroid dysfunction, particularly hypothyroidism…in addition, modest
increases in dietary iodine have been suspected to cause hyperthyroidism…”
4
When your thyroid is low, the condition is known as hypothyroidism.
When it’s too high, it’s called hyperthyroidism.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Wheat contains a nasty little thyroid-crusher called apigenin.5
Apigenin kidnaps iodine and takes it prisoner.
Whole wheat can also trigger one of the most aggressive forms of thyroid
disease called Hashimoto's Thyroiditis.
Hashimoto's Thyroiditis is an autoimmune disease.
Your body literally attacks your own thyroid gland.

Yes, most of them are.
Yet the ones I want you to avoid for 14 days are called cruciferous greens.
This most common cruciferous greens are broccoli, brussel sprouts, kale,
collard greens, and cauliflower.
The thyroid gremlin found in these greens is called thiocyanates.
Thiocyanates also interfere with your body’s iodine levels.
It prevents sufficient iodine to be absorbed…
… and that can destroy your Free T3 uptake.

Soy.
I suggest you cut soy out of your diet entirely…
...or at best eat it once a week.
Here’s why:
Soy is packed with little thyroid culprits called isoflavones…
...causing thyroid peroxidase levels to drop substantially.
Remember, this is the iodine-insertion hormone.5
And your thyroid cannot work without iodine.
That’s not all:
Soy has been shown to increase estrogen levels in both men and
women.6

There’s one more food I need to warn you about: beans.
Beans are a goitrogenic food.
Goitrogenic foods can lead to a goiter.
A goiter is a swelling in the neck caused by an enlarged thyroid gland.
You probably have no fear of getting a goiter, but goitrogenic foods can
cause inflammation of your thyroid gland.
And that’s not good.

Let’s take the last “P” first:
Pollution.
Now, there isn’t a lot you can do about the pollution in the air.
Just know that pollution is a contributing factor to thyroid problems.
Fortunately for you, it’s the least on the list.
Highest on the list are what’s known as consumable xenoestrogens.
Xenoestrogens are synthetic compounds that mimic the hormone estrogen.
If you’re a man, the last thing you want is too much estrogen.
High levels of estrogen can lead to prostate cancer, male breast tissue, and
impotence.
Yikes.
High estrogen in women can cause all sorts of problems as well…
breast cancer, early or severe menopausal symptoms, and depression.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 35 --

While environmental pollutants produce xenoestrogens in the atmosphere...
the most POTENT are those YOU INGEST DIRECTLY.
That brings us to the other 2 “Ps” of thyroid toxins:
Plastics and Parabens.
Plastics are everywhere, and HEAT activates the xenoestrogens inside it.7
Microwaved food is often in plastic containers.
We warm our baby’s milk bottle.
Foods in your cabinet may come in plastic that was under high heat during
processing.
And the list goes on.
George Bittner, professor of neurobiology at the University of Texas-Austin,
recently coauthored a paper in the NIH journal Environmental Health
Perspectives.
It reported that "almost all" commercially available plastics that were tested
leached synthetic estrogens—even when they weren't exposed to a
microwave, the steam of a dishwasher, or the sun's ultraviolet rays.8
Your best protection is to avoid heating anything in plastic.
Use a glass container instead…
….or simply put it on a plate or in a skillet with a bit of butter or coconut oil.
Avoid teflon, as it is a thyroid toxin as well.
Now, what are parabens?
Parabens are estrogen mimickers found in most beauty and bathroom
products.9
Face creams, soaps, shampoos, makeup, detergents, and wrinkle reducers
are common paraben sources.
Fortunately you can now buy paraben-free versions of each of these items.
That will go a long way to reducing these potent toxins.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

products.

Have a look at what makes The 4-Day Thyroid Diet utterly unique from
traditional diet plans:
Most diet plans work off the principle of food restriction.
The less calories you take in, the more calories you burn from fat…
… or so you’ve been led to believe.
In reality, depriving yourself of calories for too many days obliterates
thyroid function.
Here’s why:
As we evolved over the centuries, we ate in spurts.
Some days we feasted, other days we ate less.
This eating pattern is encoded into your DNA.
When food became scarce and calories low, our body’s natural defense
systems kicked in.
It started shutting down all fat-burning organs…
… especially your THYROID GLAND.
Why?
Because you needed stored body fat to survive a famine.
That’s why ANY DIET that cuts calories for too long will always fail.
Always.
You’ve literally been HARD-WIRED to eat!
Please understand:

You may have a few questions about
The 4-Day Thyroid Diet...such as:
How fast will I see and feel results?
Definitely within the first 4-Day Thyroid Nutrient Cycle… you’ll look better
and feel the difference.
Your greatest weight loss will occur within 7-14 days.
Sometimes the speed of the weight loss freaks people out… it’s pretty rapid
in many cases.
Fear not: this is normal.
Your weight loss will stabilize after about 14 days, but still continue at a
pace that will probably surprise you.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 31 of 35 --

The energy enhancement and more youthful appearance takes place over
time.
The best results are seen after about 45 days.
Is The 4-Day Thyroid Diet Safe?
It’s not only safe, it’s essential if you want to save your thyroid.
Now, I’m not saying your thyroid gland will wither away and die without it.
What I am saying is that it will probably NEVER be optimized unless you
follow the principles found within the system.
What if I lose weight too fast?
Just increase the food you eat on all 4 Thyroid Cycle days. It’s that simple.
YOU are in control.
You decide how much or how little weight you desire to lose…
… and how fast is comfortable for you.
Can my kids enjoy The 4-Day Thyroid Diet menus?
They’ll never know they are eating the most thyroid-friendly diet on the
planet… trust me!
You may even discover that feeding them becomes even easier… even for
the most picky little eaters!

I have you covered with my bonus report on the exercises to avoid…
… and the simple substitutes I recommend.
Once your Free T3 levels optimize…
… and you’ll know that when you see the weight coming off and a
younger-looking reflection staring back at you in the mirror…
… THEN you can resume any exercise plan you desire.
But you do not need to exercise to experience the thyroid renewal benefits
of The 4-Day Thyroid Diet.
I just think it’s a good idea to move in general for your overall health.
